Synthetic Canadians: Comparing Synthetic Survey Data Generators

2021 Canadian Election Study — Political Attitudes Validation Study

Author

Synthetic Opinion Lab

Published

March 14, 2026

Overview

This report validates three synthetic data generation approaches against the 2021 Canadian Election Study (CES21), combining campaign period (CPS) and post-election (PES) survey modules (n = 9,418 complete cases from 20,968 total respondents).

The 21 variables span: democratic satisfaction, political efficacy (3 items), economic evaluations (2 items), populism (3-item battery), institutional confidence (4 items), immigration attitudes (2 items), social conservatism (3 items), income inequality, party identification, and actual vote choice.

Three generators are compared:

Generator N synthetic Overall quality score
LLM (Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct) 100 0.606
IRT (Graded Response Model) 1,000 0.485
Agent (Opinion dynamics) 500 0.559

Correlation Structure

Frobenius distance from real correlation matrix:
  IRT                 : Frobenius = 4.62, r(lower tri) = 0.141
  LLM                 : Frobenius = 4.85, r(lower tri) = 0.410
  Agent               : Frobenius = 12.75, r(lower tri) = 0.185

Regression Replication: Actual Vote Predictors

Logistic regression predicting Conservative vs. Liberal actual vote from attitude items.

Summary

Dimension Real IRT LLM Agent
N respondents 9,418 1,000 100 500
Overall quality score — 0.485 0.606 0.559
Valid response range ✅ ✅ ✅ ✅
Mean-level accuracy — Partial Good Partial
Variance accuracy — Good Moderate Moderate
Correlation structure — Moderate Best Weak

Note: CES21 combines Campaign Period Survey (CPS, cps21_) and Post-Election Survey (PES, pes21_) modules. Complete cases (n=9,418) are respondents who answered both and provided substantive (non-DK) responses on all 21 selected variables. Vote choice in PES reflects actual September 20, 2021 election vote, not vote intention.

LLM achieves the highest fidelity (0.606). IRT (0.485) produces valid responses with good variance. Agent (0.559) converges to moderate intensity.
